subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Particle Motion
stimulus: Rocket A has positive velocity $v(t)$ after being launched upward from an initial height of 0 at time $t = 0$. The velocity of the rocket is recorded for selected values of $t$ over the interval $0 \le t \le 80$ seconds, as shown in the table above.

parts.prompt: Find the average acceleration of rocket A over the time interval $0 \le t \le 80$ seconds. Indicate units of measure.

Using correct units, explain the meaning of $\int_{10}^{70} v(t)\, dt$ in terms of the rocket's flight. Use a midpoint Riemann sum with 3 subintervals of equal length to approximate $\int_{10}^{70} v(t)\, dt$.

required_elements: Distance (in feet) traveled by rocket A from $t=10$ to $t=70$ seconds

scoring_points.description: Uses correct midpoint values $v(20), v(40), v(60)$

parts.prompt: Rocket B is launched upward with an acceleration of $a(t) = \dfrac{3}{\sqrt{t+1}}$ feet per second per second. At time $t = 0$ seconds, the initial height of the rocket is 0 feet, and the initial velocity is 2 feet per second. Which of the two rockets is traveling faster at time $t = 80$ seconds? Explain your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: v_B(t) = 2 + integral from 0 to t of 3/sqrt(s+1) ds. Antiderivative of 3/sqrt(s+1) = 6*sqrt(s+1). So v_B(t) = 2 + [6*sqrt(s+1)] from 0 to t = 2 + 6*sqrt(t+1) - 6 = 6*sqrt(t+1) - 4. v_B(80) = 6*sqrt(81) - 4 = 6*9 - 4 = 54 - 4 = 50. v_A(80) = 49. So Rocket B is faster.

scoring_points.description: Correct integral setup for Rocket B's velocity: $v_B(t) = 2 + \int_0^t \frac{3}{\sqrt{s+1}}\, ds$
Correct comparison and conclusion: Rocket B is traveling faster since $v_B(80) = 50 > 49 = v_A(80)$

alternatives.correct_example: Since $v_B(80) = 50 > 49 = v_A(80)$, Rocket B is traveling faster at $t = 80$ seconds.

common_traps: Not identifying the correct initial velocity (2 ft/sec, not 0)
